pi_items.source: AirNav.com - Oakland VOR/DME is the official navigation aid with ICAO identifier OAK
AirNav.com - Oakland VOR/DME coordinates: 37-43-33.332N which converts to 37.7259256 decimal degrees
AirNav.com - Oakland VOR/DME coordinates: 122-13-24.931W which converts to -122.2235919 decimal degrees
Aviation standard convention: In aviation, heading 360 degrees represents North. The FAA ATC manual specifies that north is 360 degrees, not 0 degrees.
SAR procedures documentation (IAMSAR patterns): 10 NM leg length is standard for Search and Rescue parallel track search patterns
SAR procedures documentation: 1 NM track spacing is standard for parallel track search patterns and determines the width of coverage
Pattern geometry calculation: A 6-leg parallel track pattern requires minimum 6 waypoints for the turning points of each leg
Android application structure: Standard database location for Android app package com.ds.avare (Avare aviation GPS). Avare is real open-source GPS app at apps4av.com
Android external storage standard path. /sdcard is the standard Android external storage directory. Path created by export_result.sh script which copies from internal database

privileged_info_summary: Oakland VOR (identifier OAK) is located at 37.7259°N, 122.2236°W in the San Francisco Bay Area. The task uses rounded coordinates (37.72, -122.22) accurate to within 0.36 nautical miles. Direction 360° represents North in aviation convention. The task specifies a 6-leg Search and Rescue parallel track pattern with 10 NM leg length and 1 NM track spacing, which are standard SAR parameters. The flight plan is created in the Avare aviation GPS app (com.ds.avare), a real open-source application. The task creates synthetic flight plan data during execution, stored in SQLite database plans.db at /data/data/com.ds.avare/databases/plans.db internally and exported to /sdcard/tasks/generate_sar_pattern/plans.db.
